title: Landolphia jumellei
taxonomy: Landolphia jumellei belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to Madagascar. Its classification also places it within the genus Landolphia. Further classification positions it in the order Gentianales. It is a species of the Apocynaceae family of flowering plants.

intro: Landolphia jumellei, also known as the Madagascar poison apple, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its toxic and medicinal properties. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Landolphia jumellei include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include extracts and infusions, which are used in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and other conditions.
